summary: Iteration 38 email status tracking fix VERIFIED. All 3 endpoints (send_templated_email, admin_send_email, send_member_email) now properly transition email_logs.status from 'sending' -> 'sent'/'failed' via the _send_and_update wrapper coroutine pattern. SMTP is not configured in preview, so new sends correctly report status='failed' with error='SMTP send returned False' (or 'SMTP send failed' for member email). Zero logs remain stuck on 'queued'. All 4 tests in test_email_queue_status.py pass.
critical_code_review_comments: Fix pattern is now consistent across all 3 email-sending code paths (send_templated_email, admin_send_email, send_member_email) — all use insert with status='sending' then update to 'sent'/'failed' from within a wrapper coroutine passed to background_tasks.
server.py is still >5400 lines. Recommend extracting email/notification logic into backend/services/email.py so future fixes stay consistent (also raised in iteration_37).

queue_bulk_emails still inserts a summary row with status='queued' (audit record for a batch). Currently harmless because after backfill there are 0 queued rows in logs, but consider a distinct status like 'batch_queued' to avoid confusion in stats bucket.
seed_data_creation: Added email log rows via /api/admin/email/send (subject: TEST_status_tracking_iter37 - now shows 'failed') and /api/admin/members/{id}/send-email (subject: TEST_iter38_member_email - shows 'failed'). All correctly reach terminal status.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Email status tracking is fully fixed. Stats endpoint /api/admin/email-queue/stats shows logs={'failed': 2, 'sent': 43} and queue={} — no stuck 'queued'. If SMTP gets configured on VPS, new sends should show 'sent' automatically without code changes. Test file /app/backend/tests/test_email_queue_status.py is the regression suite for this feature.
rca of the issue: Iteration 37 had fixed only send_templated_email. Iteration 38 extended the same try/except wrapper pattern to admin_send_email (line 2486) and send_member_email (line 1608). Both now (1) insert log with status='sending', (2) define an async _send_and_update() coroutine that calls send_email_smtp and updates the log row to 'sent' or 'failed' based on the return value/exception, (3) enqueue _send_and_update via background_tasks.add_task. Verified in preview: all newly-sent emails reach terminal status within a couple seconds; zero rows remain 'queued'.
